does: This paper conducts a systematic audit of how six major LLM development initiatives—GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Gemma, and others from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Meta—implement alignment in practice. Rather than focusing on computational techniques like RLHF, the authors examine the value-setting and data-centric dimensions of alignment by analyzing publicly available documentation from these organizations. The core contribution is revealing how alignment objectives are chosen, what values are embedded in models, and how datasets are selected and used to imprint these objectives. The survey provides a comprehensive empirical analysis of the scope and specificity of alignment processes across leading proprietary and open-weight LLM initiatives, offering concrete insights into the practical implementation gaps between theoretical alignment frameworks and real-world deployment.
connects: This work builds on the growing interdisciplinary discussion of AI alignment that extends beyond computational methods like RLHF to encompass philosophical and legal perspectives. It responds to earlier research on RLHF and post-training methods by shifting focus toward upstream questions: what values are being selected, how are they determined, and what data grounds these choices. The paper connects to emerging critical scholarship examining transparency and interpretability in LLM development, documentation analysis studies, and socio-technical perspectives on AI governance. It also engages with prior work on value specification, dataset bias, and the tension between alignment goals and model capabilities across different organizations.
enables: This audit provides a foundation for more rigorous accountability mechanisms in LLM development by identifying current documentation practices and gaps. It enables future work on standardized alignment reporting, comparative analysis of value choices across organizations, and development of better frameworks for data governance in model training. The systematic documentation of how different organizations approach alignment opens pathways for policy recommendations around transparency requirements, for designing audit protocols that other AI developers can adopt, and for understanding how stated alignment values translate into actual training decisions. It supports broader efforts to make the value-setting process in AI development more explicit, contestable, and democratically accountable.
